zh.lifeSummary: 當Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer於大約1538年出生在英國賀特福爾其皮三 巴內特時，她的父親Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House39歲，母親Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe33歲。她於1560年6月15日與William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment在英國賀特福爾巴內特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1601年7月23日之前在英國貝德福爾亨洛福，埋葬在英國貝德福爾亨洛福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer于大约1538年出生在英国賀特福爾其皮三 巴內特时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House39岁，母亲Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe33岁。她于1560年6月15日与William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment在英国賀特福爾巴內特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1601年7月23日之前在英国貝德福爾亨洛福，埋葬在英国貝德福爾亨洛福。

nameMeanings.name: Fischer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Gerhard, Helmut, Fritz, Klaus, Horst, Dieter, Franz, Ernst.

German, Danish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle High German visch, German Fisch ‘fish’ + the agent suffix -er. This form of the surname is also established in some other parts of Europe, notably in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, and the Netherlands. In Poland, Czechia, Croatia, and Slovenia it is (also) established in the Slavicized forms Fiszer (Polish) and Fišer (see Fiser ). Compare Fisher .

variants.name: Fischler
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Mathias, Arnd, Ernst, Erwin, Inge, Kurt, Reinhardt.  Jewish Emanuel, Nurit.
South German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Fischer  .

variants.name: Fisher
meanings: English: occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle English fis(sc)her(e) ‘fisherman’ (Old English fiscere). In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from many other languages, including German Fischer and its Slavic(ized) variant Fišer (see Fiser ), Dutch Visser , Hungarian Halász (see Halasz ), Italian Pescatore , Slovenian Ribič (see Ribic ), and Croatian Ribić or Ribar .

English: in a few cases, possibly a topographic name for someone who lived near a fish weir on a river, from Middle English fis(sc)hwere, fisshyar ‘fish weir’ (Old English fiscwer, fiscgear), or a habitational name from a place so named, such as Fisher in North Mundham, Sussex.
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Bradáin ‘descendant of Bradán’, a personal name meaning ‘salmon’. See Braden .

variants.name: Fiser
meanings: Czech, Slovenian, Croatian, and Slovak (Fišer): occupational name for a fisher, from German Fischer , often applied as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic occupational names or surnames, e.g. Slovenian Ribič (see Ribic ).
Americanized form of German Feiser, unexplained. Compare Fizer .

nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ostensibly from the vocabulary word denoting the flower (Latin rosa). However, the name was in use throughout the Middle Ages, long before any of the other girls' names derived from flowers, which are generally of 19th-century origin. In part it may refer to the flower as a symbol of the Virgin Mary, but it seems more likely that it also has a Germanic origin, probably as a short form of various girls' names based on hros ‘horse’ or hrōd ‘fame’. The Latinate form Rohesia is commonly found in documents of the Middle Ages. As well as being a name in its own right, it is currently used as a short form of Rosemary and, less often (because of their different pronunciation), of other names beginning Ros-, such as Rosalind and Rosamund .

nameMeanings.name: Marshe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Marta
meanings: contracted form of Märeta, an obsolete variant of Merete . The name is also popular in Sweden.

variants.name: Marte
meanings: New Testament name, of Aramaic rather than Hebrew origin, meaning ‘lady’. It was borne by the sister of Lazarus and Mary of Bethany (John 11:1). According to Luke 10:38, when Jesus visited the house of Mary and Martha, Mary sat at his feet, listening to him, while Martha ‘was cumbered about much serving’, so that she complained to Jesus, ‘Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone?’ For this reason, the name Martha has always been associated with hard domestic work, as opposed to the contemplative life.

variants.name: Martha
meanings: New Testament name, of Aramaic rather than Hebrew origin, meaning ‘lady’. It was borne by the sister of Lazarus and Mary of Bethany (John 11:1). According to Luke 10:38, when Jesus visited the house of Mary and Martha, Mary sat at his feet, listening to him, while Martha ‘was cumbered about much serving’, so that she complained to Jesus, ‘Lord, dost thou not care that my sister hath left me to serve alone?’ For this reason, the name Martha has always been associated with hard domestic work, as opposed to the contemplative life.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer was born about 1538, in Chipping Barnet, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House, was 39 and her mother, Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe, was 33. She married William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment on 15 June 1560, in Barnet, Hertfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died before 23 July 1601, in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, and was buried in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer est née environ 1538, à Chipping Barnet, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment le 15 juin 1560, à Barnet, Hertfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée avant 23 juillet 1601, à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, et a été enterrée à Henlow, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer um 1538 in Chipping Barnet, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House 39 und ihre Mutter Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe 33. Sie hat am 15. Juni 1560 in Barnet, Hertfordshire, England William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb vor 23. Juli 1601 in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England und wurde in Henlow, Bedfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer è nata il circa 1538 nel Chipping Barnet, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House, aveva 39 anni e sua madre, Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment il 15 giugno 1560, nel Barnet, Hertfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì prima del 23 luglio 1601, nel Henlow, Bedfordshire, England, e fu sepolta a Henlow, Bedfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Rose Marshe Agnes Fischerが約1538年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアチップイング バーネットで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy Houseは39歳，母Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marsheは33歳であった。1560年6月15日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアバーネットでWilliam Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parlimentと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1601年7月23日以前にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウで死去し，イギリスベドフォードシュアイアヘンロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer(이)가 대략 1538년 영국 허트퍼드실르 치핀그 바른에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House(은)는 39세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1560년 6월 15일 영국 허트퍼드실르 바른에서 William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1601년 7월 23일 이전 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에서 사망했으며 영국 베드퍼드실르 헨로에 묻혔다.

pt.from 3 June 1570 to 30 June 1571: de 3 de junho de 1570 a 30 de junho de 1571
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer nasceu aproximadamente 1538, em Chipping Barnet, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment em 15 de junho de 1560, em Barnet, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 23 de julho de 1601, em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, e foi sepultada em Henlow, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rose Marshe Agnes Fischer родилась около 1538 г. в местности чиппинг барнет, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Richard Robert Marshe of Dey or Dairy House было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Agnes Walker Marshe, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Hurst Baron of Henlow, Member of Parliment 15 июня 1560 г. в местности барнет, хиртфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла перед 23 июля 1601 г. в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия и была похоронена в местности хенлоу, бедфордшир, Англия.
